executive_summary: Weekly sales inc VAT closed at 370,022.76 against an ex-VAT figure of 340,421.20, reflecting a solid trading week for Skerries store 424.

Overall gross margin of 17.39% requires close monitoring; department-level P&L data is showing anomalous cost of sales figures across all fresh and ambient categories due to missing purchases and opening stock inputs, which is distorting reported margins significantly and must be investigated before these figures are used for any financial submission.
Total waste of 8,191.04 represents 2.41% of ex-VAT sales, which is above an acceptable threshold for a store of this size and warrants immediate departmental review.
Fish waste of 1,625.36 and Produce waste of 1,763.83 are the two largest individual waste contributors and together account for 41.4% of total store waste this week.
Provisions waste of 1,016.80 and Deli waste of 1,204.23 are also significant, combining for a further 27.1% of total waste.

Cigarettes department is reporting a closing stock of 123,650.74 with zero purchases and zero opening stock recorded, producing a nonsensical margin of 1,631.18%; this is a data integrity issue that must be resolved with the bookkeeper immediately.

All ten departments in the P&L summary are showing negative cost of sales figures, confirming that purchase and opening stock data has not been correctly posted for this week; the overall margin of 17.39% from the summary figures should be used as the reliable reference point until restated figures are available.
biggest_wins: Produce delivered the highest sales contribution in the fresh categories at 40,960.14 ex VAT, with a scan margin of 19.47%, indicating strong throughput and pricing compliance at the shelf.
Poultry posted sales of 12,936.92 with a scan margin of 15.64%, representing healthy volume for the category and suggesting promotional activity or strong consumer demand this week.
Deli achieved sales of 8,464.12 with the highest scan margin across all departments at 42.12%, indicating strong made-to-order and hot food performance relative to cost of ingredients.
Provisions recorded sales of 24,532.33, the second highest department by value in the fresh and semi-fresh grouping, with a scan margin of 19.20%, showing consistent everyday grocery performance.
Beef generated sales of 12,633.46 with a scan margin of 19.00%, a solid result for the butchery counter and in line with expected category performance.
biggest_risks: Data integrity is the primary risk this week: purchases and opening stock are missing for every department in the P&L, rendering all reported cost of sales, gross profit, and margin figures unreliable. These must be corrected before any period-end reporting.

Cigarettes department shows a closing stock of 123,650.74 with no purchases or opening stock posted, producing a reported margin of 1,631.18%. This figure is clearly erroneous and requires urgent investigation to confirm stock is physically present and correctly valued.

Fish waste of 1,625.36 against sales of 5,599.55 implies a waste-to-sales ratio of approximately 29.0% for the category, which is critically high and unsustainable. Order quantities, display life, and markdown procedures must be reviewed immediately.

Produce waste of 1,763.83 against sales of 40,960.14 gives a category waste rate of approximately 4.3%, which is above acceptable norms and indicates over-ordering or insufficient markdown activity during the latter part of the week.
Deli waste of 1,204.23 against sales of 8,464.12 represents approximately 14.2% waste-to-sales for the department. Production planning and end-of-day clearance protocols need to be tightened.
Provisions waste of 1,016.80 is notable given the category's ambient and chilled nature. Date code management and rotation discipline should be audited on the shop floor.

Poultry waste of 648.74 against sales of 12,936.92 equates to approximately 5.0% waste-to-sales, which is elevated for a protein category and may indicate over-ordering or insufficient promotional activity to clear short-dated lines.
Bread and cakes waste of 764.78 suggests bakery ordering and bake-off scheduling is not sufficiently aligned with daily sales patterns, particularly toward the end of the week.
department_callouts: Cigarettes: Closing stock of 123,650.74 posted with no purchases or opening stock; reported margin of 1,631.18% is invalid. Immediate stock reconciliation and correct purchase posting required. Scan margin of 12.82% is the only reliable reference point.
Beef: Sales of 12,633.46 with scan margin of 19.00%; waste of 293.31 is manageable at approximately 2.3% of sales. P&L cost of sales figure is unreliable due to missing stock inputs.

Lamb: Sales of 1,249.36 with scan margin of 25.56%, the highest scan margin in the butchery group. Waste of 100.36 is acceptable relative to volume. Category is small but performing well on theoretical margin.

Pork: Sales of 1,363.07 with scan margin of 22.66%; waste of 154.17 equates to approximately 11.3% of sales, which is high for the category and should be reviewed against order quantities and display life.

Bacon and Loose SRP: Sales of 1,010.73 with a scan margin of only 11.91%, the lowest scan margin in the butchery and deli grouping. Waste of 109.02 at approximately 10.8% of sales is also a concern. Pricing and wastage controls need attention.
Poultry: Sales of 12,936.92 with scan margin of 15.64%; waste of 648.74 is approximately 5.0% of sales. Order review and markdown strategy recommended to reduce end-of-week surplus.

Fish: Sales of 5,599.55 with scan margin of 14.51%; waste of 1,625.36 equates to approximately 29.0% of sales. This is the most critical waste issue in the store this week and requires an immediate response on ordering, display, and markdown.

Produce: Sales of 40,960.14 with scan margin of 19.47%; waste of 1,763.83 equates to approximately 4.3% of sales. While the department leads on turnover, waste reduction through better ordering and more aggressive markdown is required.

Deli: Sales of 8,464.12 with scan margin of 42.12%, reflecting strong theoretical margin from hot food and made-to-order. Waste of 1,204.23 at approximately 14.2% of sales is the main concern. Production scheduling and batch sizing should be reviewed.
Provisions: Sales of 24,532.33 with scan margin of 19.20%; waste of 1,016.80 is relatively high for an ambient-leaning category. Date code management and FIFO rotation should be audited.
action_plan: Escalate P&L data issue to the store bookkeeper and regional accounts team today: all department purchases and opening stock figures must be correctly posted so that accurate cost of sales and gross margin can be calculated for week ending 2026-06-06.

Conduct an immediate physical stock count and purchase reconciliation for the Cigarettes department to explain the 123,650.74 closing stock figure and verify there is no stock discrepancy or posting error.

Fish department: Review the ordering schedule for the coming week with the fish buyer or supplier. Implement a daily waste log with target quantities. Introduce a structured end-of-day markdown at least two hours before close to clear short-dated stock and reduce the 1,625.36 weekly waste.

Produce department: Analyse daily waste logs to identify which lines are driving the 1,763.83 weekly waste figure. Reduce order quantities on the top three waste lines by a minimum of 15% for the coming week and implement a daily afternoon markdown on short-dated items.

Deli department: Review production schedules for hot food and prepared lines. Reduce batch sizes for the last production run of the day and introduce a 30% markdown on all lines entering the final two hours of trade to drive clearance and reduce the 1,204.23 weekly waste.

Provisions department: Arrange a full date code check across chilled and ambient Provisions lines this week. Identify any lines contributing disproportionately to the 1,016.80 waste figure and adjust order quantities or rotation procedures accordingly.

Poultry department: Review weekly order quantities against the sales figure of 12,936.92 and waste figure of 648.74. Ensure markdown of short-dated poultry is actioned by midday on the day prior to use-by date to maximise sell-through.

Bacon and Loose SRP: The scan margin of 11.91% is the lowest in the store's fresh grouping. Review pricing on key lines against the planogram and check for any scanning or pricing errors that may be suppressing the theoretical margin.

Bread and Cakes: Waste of 764.78 indicates bake-off scheduling is not aligned with daily demand. Reduce afternoon bake quantities on Thursday, Friday, and Saturday and review daily sales data to right-size production by day of week.

Pork department: Waste of 154.17 against sales of 1,363.07 is approximately 11.3% of sales. Review display quantities, packaging dates, and markdown timing to bring waste in line with the beef category benchmark of approximately 2.3%.

Dairy and Frozen: While not in the department P&L due to missing data, dairy waste of 284.52 and frozen waste of 225.93 should be reviewed with the relevant section managers to confirm rotation and ordering are being managed correctly.

Request a corrected and fully populated P&L from the bookkeeper by midweek so that a reliable overall margin figure, department-level gross profit, and cash margin can be reviewed at the next management meeting and compared against the scan margin benchmarks.
